text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Are there any quota / reserved seats? Answer: There are no quota seats.
title: NUST FAQ: Are there any quota / reserved seats?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Are there any reserved / quota seats for admission in MBBS Programme? Answer: Admissions in NSHS will be purely on merit , based upon the MDCAT conducted by NUMS .
title: NUST FAQ: Are there any reserved / quota seats for admission in MBBS Programme?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can a candidate apply both for open merit and ACT based seats? Answer: Yes, on the basis of NET / MDCAT Score and required ACT Score.
title: NUST FAQ: Can a candidate apply both for open merit and ACT based seats?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can a candidate of ICS / equivalent qualification eligible to apply for Engineering programmes? Answer: Yes, as per PEC regulation No PEC/EAD/46-VCCM/Regulations/2023 dated 30 March 2023, candidates having combination of Physics, Mathematics and Computer Studies / Computer Science (ICS) or equivalent are eligible to apply for all the Engineering programs offered by NUST, but they have to clear Chemistry as a remedial subject / course in the 1st Semester after admission. Candidates, who already appeared in any NET series during current admission cycle, will also be able to update their choice preferences while applying for Series-4 or after the announcement of merit, before start of the selection process.
title: NUST FAQ: Can a candidate of ICS / equivalent qualification eligible to apply for Engineer

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can a candidate with FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification, appeared in additional mathematics paper and waiting for the result, apply for admission in Engineering programs on the basis of Pre Medical marks? Answer: Candidate can apply on the basis of Pre-Medical result, however he / she has to provide the additional math result with passing marks and overall 60% marks in HSSC at the time of joining NUST.
title: NUST FAQ: Can a candidate with FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification, appeared in add

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can candidates of FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification apply for BS Computer Science? Answer: Yes, but they have to clear deficient Mathematics courses of 6 credit hours, within one year of enrollment in the programme.
title: NUST FAQ: Can candidates of FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification apply for BS Comput

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can foreign / international students apply for admission in BSHND programme? Answer: Foreign students are eligible to apply on the basis of NET-Applied Sciences / NUMS-MDCAT / National MDCAT / ACT Test.
title: NUST FAQ: Can foreign / international students apply for admission in BSHND programme?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can foreign students apply for admission at NSHS? Answer: Foreign students can apply to MBBS Programme at NSHS on the basis of NUMS MDCAT, SAT-II or foreign MCAT / UCAT score.
title: NUST FAQ: Can foreign students apply for admission at NSHS?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can I apply for computing programmes with HSSC / A level background in Arts / Humanities group? Answer: Yes, any candidate who has Mathematics as one of the mandatory subjects in HSSC / A level / Equivalent, is eligible to apply for computing programmes.
title: NUST FAQ: Can I apply for computing programmes with HSSC / A level background in Arts / Hu

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can I apply for more than one programme? Answer: Yes, if you meet the eligibility criteria.
title: NUST FAQ: Can I apply for more than one programme?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can I apply for rechecking of Entry Test Result? Answer: Re-checking of Paper Based Entry Test may be requested within 5 days of the declaration of result along with a fee of Rs.500/- in the form of a bank draft/pay order in favour of NUST. The original bank draft / pay order should be dispatched to Admissions Directorate, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad, along with written application. Re-checking involves the verification of paper for any unmarked answers and error free totaling of marks.
title: NUST FAQ: Can I apply for rechecking of Entry Test Result?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can I get a copy of NUST Entry Test result? Answer: Desirous candidates may obtain the result details of their Test from the link available on NUST website.
title: NUST FAQ: Can I get a copy of NUST Entry Test result?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Can I get some sample Test papers of NUST Entry Test? Answer: Mock Test for Computer Based / Paper Based Test are available on NUST website www.ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk under the link Sample Test , for candidates to practice.
title: NUST FAQ: Can I get some sample Test papers of NUST Entry Test?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Do I fall under the Expatriate Students category? Answer: If you are temporarily or permanently residing in a country other than Pakistan, and hold either a Pakistani passport, or dual nationality (one of them being Pakistani), you fall under the Expatriate Student category. For details, please click here .
title: NUST FAQ: Do I fall under the Expatriate Students category?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Does NUST cater for the pick and drop facility for the students? Answer: Pick and drop service is provided to students of Rawalpindi and Islamabad only with specified routes , for which the student has to pay separate charges. You will be guided to avail the facility during the orientation session.
title: NUST FAQ: Does NUST cater for the pick and drop facility for the students?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Does NUST cater for the pick and drop facility? Answer: Facility is available for the residents of Rawalpindi and Islamabad with specified routes , for which they have to pay separate charges. Details will be shared during the orientation session.
title: NUST FAQ: Does NUST cater for the pick and drop facility?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Does NUST offer scholarship / financial assistance? Answer: Information regarding scholarships offered by NUST can be accessed by clicking here. Students showing good academic performance by maintaining a minimum GPA of 3.5 out of 4.0 in each semester, are eligible to get financial assistance, as per policy.
title: NUST FAQ: Does NUST offer scholarship / financial assistance?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: For various academics backgrounds, which options for various UG disciplines are available? Answer: The options available to candidates for admission in various UG programmes according to their academic background are accessible by visiting following link:- https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/academic-qualification-required-for-different-ug-progammes/
title: NUST FAQ: For various academics backgrounds, which options for various UG disciplines are

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: How can I apply at NUST if I am an expatriate student? Answer: An expatriate having dual nationality can opt for both NET and SAT on national and international seats.
title: NUST FAQ: How can I apply at NUST if I am an expatriate student?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: How can I apply for admission in BS Human Nutrition and Dietetics (BSHND)? Answer: To apply online for admission in BSHND, please click on the following link: http://nshsadmission.nust.edu.pk/hnd/
title: NUST FAQ: How can I apply for admission in BS Human Nutrition and Dietetics (BSHND)?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: How can I apply for admission in MBBS Programme? Answer: For information regarding MBBS admission in NSHS, please click the following link: https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/NSHS/how-to-apply/
title: NUST FAQ: How can I apply for admission in MBBS Programme?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: How can I submit the application processing fee (online) using 1Link option? Answer: Candidates can use the unique 17-digit 1Bill Invoice / Consumer No. mentioned under the Fee Invoice section , to pay their fee via 1Link service through any of the following payment modes: Mobile App / Online Banking (Any Bank) ATM (Any Bank) Easy Paisa / Jazz Cash etc. The benefit of making payment through 1Link is that you don t have to wait for fee verification because it is verified instantly.
title: NUST FAQ: How can I submit the application processing fee (online) using 1Link option?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: How can I submit the application processing fee? Answer: Candidates can submit the fee through any of the following options :- 1Link (1Bill Invoice Number) Credit Card
title: NUST FAQ: How can I submit the application processing fee?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: How many allied programmes does NSHS offer? Answer: Currently, NSHS is offering admission to BS Human Nutrition and Dietetics (BSHND).
title: NUST FAQ: How many allied programmes does NSHS offer?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: I am a born Pakistani with a foreign passport; can I apply as an expatriate student? Answer: If you don't have Pakistani nationality like having CNIC / NICOP, you can apply as an international student (foreign student). You can apply on the basis of NET or ACT/SAT or both as per eligibility criteria but have to pay tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for foreign nationals. If you are a POC holder and applying on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT national seat, you will be paying tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for national student.
title: NUST FAQ: I am a born Pakistani with a foreign passport; can I apply as an expatriate stud

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: I am a foreigner and want to get admission at NUST? Answer: If you fulfill the eligibility criteria, you can apply for admission on the basis of NET or ACT / SAT (International seat) or both as per policy in vogue. For details, visit https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/international-students/ .
title: NUST FAQ: I am a foreigner and want to get admission at NUST?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: I am an overseas Pakistani who has completed O & A level from abroad, do I have to pass Urdu, Islamiyat / Ethics, and Pakistan Studies to get IBCC equivalence? Answer: No, there isn t any such compulsory requirement of passing the above-mentioned subjects, as per IBCC equivalence rules. For further details, please visit IBCC website: https://ibcc.edu.pk/rules-for-equivalence/
title: NUST FAQ: I am an overseas Pakistani who has completed O & A level from abroad, do I have

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: I am in my final year of A Level and my result will be announced in August 2024. Can I apply? Answer: Yes, you can apply, as in such case you will be evaluated on the basis of O levels equivalence marks but you have to provide equivalence certificate of A level within 15 days of announcement of result or at the time of admission.
title: NUST FAQ: I am in my final year of A Level and my result will be announced in August 2024.

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: I could not appear in NUST Entry Test, Can I take the Test on some other day? Answer: NUST is conducting NET in paper based format on one day and in computer based format in two sessions daily, on different days, as per the schedule. As per policy, if a candidate misses the Test session he / she is not given another chance. But in case of extreme emergency, requests may be entertained on case to case basis, depending on availability of space.
title: NUST FAQ: I could not appear in NUST Entry Test, Can I take the Test on some other day?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: I have DAE background. Which programmes can I apply for? Answer: DAE candidate should have completed DAE with minimum 60% marks in Matric as well as DAE The detail of Engineering programmes offered to DAE candidates as approved by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) can be accessed by visiting https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/dae-eligibility-criteria/ .
title: NUST FAQ: I have DAE background. Which programmes can I apply for?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: If I am selected on all 3 merits, what should I do? Answer: If selected on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT on national as well as international seat, you have the option to retain any one seat of your own choice.
title: NUST FAQ: If I am selected on all 3 merits, what should I do?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: If I have taken ACT / SAT Tests, do I still have to sit in the Entry Test? Answer: Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for applying on the basis of ACT / SAT and NET. Those applying on ACT / SAT basis does not require to appear in NET, however they can opt for NET in addition to ACT /SAT specified seats. In this way, they will be considered for both the streams.
title: NUST FAQ: If I have taken ACT / SAT Tests, do I still have to sit in the Entry Test?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Is a candidate having less than 60% marks in FSc part-1 eligible to apply for admission in NUST? Answer: Yes, the marks entered for FSc part-1 will be utilized for merit generation. However on joining NUST, the candidate must have secure 60% or more marks in FSc complete result.
title: NUST FAQ: Is a candidate having less than 60% marks in FSc part-1 eligible to apply for ad

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Is an equivalence certificate of qualification other than Matric & FSc / FA necessary? Can I not submit my O'level and A' level certificates? (I ask so, because I will be applying from abroad)? Answer: Equivalence certificates duly issued by IBCC Pakistan are necessary for those candidates who do not possess Matric & FSc / FA qualifications. Submission of only O&A level grade / mark sheets will not serve the purpose. All non FSc stream candidates must have equivalence certificate of their qualification, duly obtained from IBCC, Pakistan in relevant groups/subjects and with minimum 60% marks.
title: NUST FAQ: Is an equivalence certificate of qualification other than Matric & FSc / FA nece

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Is migration to NUST allowed? Answer: Yes, as per migration policy, students can apply for migration after completion of first year and before the beginning of final year. However, applicants with exceptionally good academic record are considered for migration to NUST. For detailed migration policy click here .
title: NUST FAQ: Is migration to NUST allowed?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Is there any age limit for Undergraduate Admission? Answer: There is no age limit for Undergraduate Admission.
title: NUST FAQ: Is there any age limit for Undergraduate Admission?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Is there any installment plan for the payment of tuition fee? Answer: As per PM&DC Regulations, a student can pay the tuition fee in installments on a quarterly , six monthly or annual basis , with a discount of 0% on quarterly, 2% on six monthly and 4% on annual basis. Necessary details will be incorporated in the fee invoice.
title: NUST FAQ: Is there any installment plan for the payment of tuition fee?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Is there any negative marking in the NUST Entry Test? Answer: No, there is no negative marking.
title: NUST FAQ: Is there any negative marking in the NUST Entry Test?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Name on my CNIC and Passport is different from that on my academic documents (O / A Level) or equivalence certificate. Which name should I use to apply for admission? Answer: For this, you have to get your name changed on your CNIC and Passport, because University will use the name as per your Matric or O level equivalence certificate (SSC), duly issued by IBCC.
title: NUST FAQ: Name on my CNIC and Passport is different from that on my academic documents (O

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: NSHS is affiliated to which University? Answer: NSHS is a constituent school of NUST. The MBBS degree will be awarded by NUST.
title: NUST FAQ: NSHS is affiliated to which University?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Question: How frequently are University fee rates revised? Answer: University fees are usually announced once each year, for the following academic year. However, fees are subject to change without notice.
title: NUST FAQ: Question: How frequently are University fee rates revised?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What are the different Undergraduate Programmes offered at NUST? Answer: For information regarding offered UG Programmes, click here.
title: NUST FAQ: What are the different Undergraduate Programmes offered at NUST?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What are the subjects for Entry Test for different categories of candidates? Answer: https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/subjects-included-in-net-with-weightings/
title: NUST FAQ: What are the subjects for Entry Test for different categories of candidates?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What category of candidates can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT? Answer: Any candidate can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT provided he / she has score for the prescribed disciplines of study. Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for this purpose. Admission on ACT / SAT basis has two categories, i.e National and International. Candidates admitted on National seats pay tuition fee and allied charges in Pak currency whereas candidates admitted against International seats pay the required dues at rates defined for international students. For admission against international seats, the following categories will be eligible:- Pakistani national students having passed an examination equivalent to intermediate level of Pakistan from a foreign education system. Foreigners and Pakistanis having dual nationality, irrespective of the place of their HSSC study, i.e Pakistan or abroad. Pakistani students who studied abroad but have passed the requisite examination from a Pakistani board.
title: NUST FAQ: What category of candidates can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What documents are essentially required to be submitted while applying for admission? Answer: While applying for admission, possession of Computerized National Identity Card (CNIC) / Form 'B' or NICOP is mandatory. Online registration system does not require attachment of documents / credentials. However, the same will be required for verification during selection and admission process of selected candidates.
title: NUST FAQ: What documents are essentially required to be submitted while applying for admis

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the admission criteria for a gap year / repeater candidates? Answer: Gap year / repeater candidates are being considered as normal candidates and there is no penalization while generating the merit.
title: NUST FAQ: What is the admission criteria for a gap year / repeater candidates?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the admission criteria for BSHND programme? Answer: The detailed admission criteria is given on the following link: https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/alliedprogram/admission-criteria/
title: NUST FAQ: What is the admission criteria for BSHND programme?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the admission schedule for BSHND programme? Answer: https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/alliedprogram/dates-to-remember/
title: NUST FAQ: What is the admission schedule for BSHND programme?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the application processing fee for online registration for admission? Answer: Application processing fee will be charged according to the following structure: NUST Entry Test (per exam) Pakistani / Dual Nationality Holder - Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/- Foreign National (Non Pakistani) - Rs. 10,000/- or USD 80/- For ACT / SAT Candidates ACT / SAT based National seat - Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/- ACT / SAT based International seat - Rs. 10,000/- or USD 80/- Application processing fee is neither refundable nor transferable . It will remain valid only for the series for which it has been paid.
title: NUST FAQ: What is the application processing fee for online registration for admission?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the deadline for submission of ACT / SAT score? Answer: Last date for ACT / SAT score to reach NUST directly from College Board, USA, is 25 July, 2025. NUST institutional code to receive ACT scores is 2915 and of SAT scores is 2790. ACT / SAT scores are valid for two years only. It is the responsibility of candidates to ensure the provision of ACT / SAT scores to Undergraduate Section, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad by the given deadline.
title: NUST FAQ: What is the deadline for submission of ACT / SAT score?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the detail of ACT / SAT Score required for admission? Answer: NUST requires ACT / SAT scores for admission in undergraduate programmes as per the following link:- https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/applying-on-the-basis-of-scholastic-aptitude-test-sat/
title: NUST FAQ: What is the detail of ACT / SAT Score required for admission?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the duration of test and the number of MCQs to be attempted? Answer: There are total 200 MCQs to be attempted in three hours (180 minutes) .
title: NUST FAQ: What is the duration of test and the number of MCQs to be attempted?

title: NUST FAQ: What is the Merit Generation Criteria for admission in MBBS Programme?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the merit generation criteria for BSHND programme? Answer: Merit details are as under: NET / MDCAT / ACT Test * - 75% HSSC (Pre-Medical) / A level / Equivalent - 15% SSC / O level / Equivalent - 10% * For ACT Based International Seats, 100% weightage will be given to required ACT Score.
title: NUST FAQ: What is the merit generation criteria for BSHND programme?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the syllabus of Entry Test subjects and format of questions? Answer: NUST Entry Test is primarily based on SSC / HSSC level studies (Part-1 & Part-II) and questions are in the form of MCQs with four answer options each. The paper of English is on the pattern of SAT exam. Sample Test . Textbooks are consulted while developing the question bank. Grasping the concepts available in different textbooks will be beneficial. https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/subjects-included-in-net-with-weightings
title: NUST FAQ: What is the syllabus of Entry Test subjects and format of questions?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the test venue for NET in different cities? Answer: In Islamabad , NET is conducted in NUST Exam Hall (Entry through Gate - 4) located inside the NUST Main Campus, Sector H-12, Islamabad. Pin location: https://rb.gy/j0b5ds In Quetta , NET is conducted at NUST Balochistan Campus (NBC). Pin Location: https://rb.gy/9ntumc In Karachi , and Gilgit , the test venue is mentioned on respective admit cards.
title: NUST FAQ: What is the test venue for NET in different cities?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: What is the timeline for the announcement of NET result? Answer: Result of computer-based NET is uploaded within 24 hours and for paper-based NET, within 5 to 7 working days . Result can be viewed by entering the Roll No. in the following link: https://ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk/result/default.aspx
title: NUST FAQ: What is the timeline for the announcement of NET result?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: When will the admission for NSHS be advertised? Answer: Admissions will be announced in electronic and print media as per NUMS and PM&DC Policy.
title: NUST FAQ: When will the admission for NSHS be advertised?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: When will the classes for MBBS programme start? Answer: Classes for MBBS programme will commence as per PM&DC guidelines.
title: NUST FAQ: When will the classes for MBBS programme start?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Where is NSHS located? Answer: NSHS is located inside the NUST main campus, sector H-12, Islamabad. The link to its pin location (on Google Maps) is: https://rb.gy/f6a26

title: NUST FAQ: Where is NUST School of Health Sciences (NSHS) located?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Which MDCAT will be valid for admission in NSHS? Answer: You can apply for admission on the basis of NUMS MDCAT only.

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Who is exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee? Answer: First, second and third position holders in HSSC exams of different BISEs are exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee. Such candidates should contact NUST through email at [email protected] along with valid proof obtained from the concerned education board.
title: NUST FAQ: Who is exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Will the admission be confirmed in case of quarterly or six-monthly payment of tuition fee? Answer: The student will be admitted to the MBBS programme on payment of tuition fee in quarterly or six-monthly installments.

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Will there be hostel facility available for the MBBS students? Answer: The facility will be available both for boys and girls as per the NUST SoP. The students are requested to apply for the same on confirmation of selection. For the details regarding hostel charges and to apply online, visit the following links: https://campuslife.nust.edu.pk/facility-and-amenity/housing-and-dining/
title: NUST FAQ: Will there be hostel facility available for the MBBS students?

text: FAQ Category: FAQs Question: Would the admission processing fee / security deposit be refundable in case a student does not join the University? Answer: The university will refund only the security deposit to the candidates who do not join the university. Admission Processing Fee is non-refundable.
